rules: Write unit tests for new features
API requests must always fetch data only for the current tenant: for logged-in users, use the tenant from their token; for public routes, provide tenant via X-Tenant-ID header.
Replace PrimeNG components with Bootstrap wherever possible; aim to remove PrimeNG from the project.
Always keep security in mind: follow best practices for authentication, authorization, data validation, and protection of sensitive information.
When adding a new API endpoint, always run Orval to generate the corresponding frontend service.
If a DB model change is made on the API, also update the sync and setup DB scripts to reflect the changes.
Always separate TypeScript (.ts) and HTML (.html) files for Angular components; do not use inline templates.
Reference the APP_DESCRIPTION.md file at least once every hour to stay up-to-date with project context and guidelines.
If new functionality is added that should be documented, update the APP_DESCRIPTION.md file to keep it current.
